text: During laparoscopic exploration, a fistula is visible between the terminal ileum and the sigmoid colon in a 52-year-old woman with Crohn's disease. The surgeon carefully dissects the fistula using a combination of sharp and blunt techniques, focusing on the terminal ileum side, which will be part of the removed specimen.

The surgeon approximates the defect in the sigmoid mesocolon using intracorporeal sutures. A flexible sigmoidoscopy is performed to visually inspect for any full-thickness defects in the sigmoid colon, followed by an air-water leak test.

The surgeon completes the ileocecal resection using a medial approach, fully mobilizing the hepatic flexure from below. The duodenum, pancreas, and gallbladder are clearly visible through the medial window. An end-to-end ileocolic anastomosis is performed on the chronically dilated small bowel.
